messages.content:
summarize this in 80 words:
The Flexible Job-Shop Production (FJSP) model constitutes a well-known generalized job-shop model where operations are performed on a group of versatile machines. The FJSP literature has been explored by various researchers. Brucker and Schlie (1990) presented a polynomial time algorithm to solve the FJSP. Brandimarte (1993) proposed a two-tier Tabu search approach to minimize completion time. Similarly, Hurink et al. (1994) suggested another Tabu search algorithm. Choi and Choi (2002) introduced mixed integer optimization for FJSP with a sequence-dependent setup. Kacem et al. (2000) developed three dispatching rules and used advanced genetic manipulation to improve the solution. Chan et al. (2006) proposed a resource constraint to minimize machine idle time. Gao et al. (2008) used a genetic algorithm and variable neighborhood descent to minimize the manufacturing time interval, the maximum machine workload, and the total workload. Pezzella et al. (2008) introduced and analyzed an improved genetic algorithm, and Xing et al. (2010) developed a hybrid algorithm based on the ant colony optimization algorithm to obtain the optimal make-span. Chen et al. (2008) studied an industrial sample in a weapons factory using FJSP.
Several studies have proposed solutions to the flexible job-shop scheduling problem (FJSP), which involves multiple products, parallel machines, and set-up times. Chan and Choy (2011) suggested a genetic algorithm-based job scheduler, while Mahdavi et al. (2010) developed a decision support system using industrial data. Zhang et al. (2011) proposed a genetic algorithm that used global and local selection strategies. Al-Turki et al. (2011) suggested different dispatching rules for FJSP with setup time, batch processing, and uncertain data. Xiong and Fu (2018) designed a multi-agent system for FJSP, while Gao et al. (2015) developed effective exploratory methods to minimize the maximum workload and total workload of machines. Ahmadi et al. (2016) developed an evolutionary algorithm to optimize multi-objective FJSP considering random machine failure.

The Flexible Job-Shop Production (FJSP) encompasses versatile machinery for various operations. Researchers have developed numerous solution methods. Brucker and Schlie offered a polynomial time algorithm in 1990. Many others, including Brandimarte and Hurink, used Tabu search, while Choi, Brandimarte, and others employed mixed integer optimization and genetic algorithms. Later, approaches like decision support systems, hybrid algorithms, and multi-agent systems have been utilized. Industrial applications have been studied, and adaptive strategies for set-up times, batch processing, and machine failure have been proposed to optimize scheduling efficiency.

summarize this : Another area of focus is lot-sizing in remanufacturing systems, specifically Dynamic Lot Sizing with Product Returns and Remanufacturing (DLSPR). This problem involves determining the optimal production quantities for both new and remanufactured products while taking into account the uncertainty and variability of product returns.

Dynamic Lot Sizing with Product Returns and Remanufacturing (DLSPR) is a problem that addresses the optimal production quantities for new and remanufactured items, factoring in the unpredictable and variable nature of returned products.

summarize this : Several methods for solving lot-sizing problems with capacity constraints have been categorized and reviewed by Buschkuhl et al. (2010), while few studies have focused on lot-sizing in remanufacturing systems, known as Dynamic Lot Sizing with Product Returns and Remanufacturing (DLSPR). Richter and Sombrutzki (2000) provide a mathematical formulation for DLSP in remanufacturing and hybrid production/remanufacturing systems. Golany et al. (2001) studied the production planning of a single product system with reproduction and disposal options

Buschkuhl et al. (2010) have reviewed various methods for solving lot-sizing problems with capacity constraints, but research specifically on lot-sizing for remanufacturing systems, or DLSPR, has been limited. Richter and Sombrutzki (2000) and Golany et al. (2001) have contributed to this field by providing mathematical formulations for DLSP in remanufacturing and analyzing single product systems with reproduction and disposal options, respectively.

summarize this in one line: Baki et al. (2014) introduced a mathematical formulation for DLSPR and developed a dynamic programming method to solve it. Sifaleras et al. (2015) presented a variable neighborhood search for DLSP with product return and recycling. Mehdizadeh and Fatehi (2017) proposed an algorithm for solving a fuzzy model to determine the lot size of a single-machine system. Notably, all these works assume no capacity constraints on lot-sizing with product return and remanufacturing.
Baki et al., Sifaleras et al., and Mehdizadeh and Fatehi proposed various algorithms for DLSPR and recycling without assuming capacity constraints.

summarize this in one line: Soleimaninia and Mehdizadeh (2018) studied the flexible job-shop scheduling by employing reverse flows approach, which consists of two flows of jobs at each stage in opposite directions. The first one going from first stage to last stage, and the second flow going from last stage to first stage. A mathematical model of problem was provided to minimize the maximal completion time of the jobs (i.e., the makespan).
Soleimaninia and Mehdizadeh (2018) developed a mathematical model for flexible job-shop scheduling using a reverse flow approach to minimize job completion times.

summarize this in one line: Rohaninejad (2015) proposed a General Lot Sizing and Scheduling Problem (GLSP) for flexible job-shop production. Roshani et al. (2015) focused on simultaneous lot-sizing and scheduling optimization in flexible job-shop production, creating a hybrid integer programming model that combined genetic and particle swarm optimization algorithms.
Rohaninejad and Roshani et al. (2015) introduced models and hybrid optimization techniques to solve lot-sizing and scheduling in flexible job-shop production.
Rohaninejad et al. (2016) also examined the integrated lot-sizing and flexible job-shop scheduling problem, using a metaheuristics method based on genetic and particle swarm optimization algorithms.
Rohaninejad et al. (2016) explored integrated lot-sizing and scheduling in job-shops, employing a metaheuristics method that combines genetic and particle swarm optimization.

summarize this in 15 words: Sahraeian et al. (2017) proposed an integrated problem of lot-sizing and flexible job-shop production with limited machine capacity, using a small-bucket model and a new harmony search algorithm. A mixed integer programming model was also utilized in this study.
Sahraeian et al. (2017) combined lot-sizing and job-shop scheduling using harmony search and mixed integer programming.

summarize this (keep all the parentheses): In their study, Zarrouk et al. (2019) propose a two-stage particle swarm optimization algorithm to address the flexible job-shop scheduling problem. The first stage is responsible for determining machine operations, while the second stage determines the operation sequence. To minimize the number of visited solutions, the authors implement a boundary-checking strategy based on the objective function's value. This algorithm is evaluated against standard problems with existing algorithms, demonstrating acceptable performance. Zhang et al. (2020) designed a new two-stage framework that selects important scheduling features for the flexible job-shop scheduling problem, achieving good scheduling with fewer decision variables. Hajibabaei and Behnamian (2021) investigate the impact of flexible resources on the flexible job-shop scheduling problem with parallel machines and sequence-dependent setup times. They use a linear programming model to minimize production, delays, delivery time, and inventory costs, and compare results with genetic algorithms after utilizing Tabu Search for large problems.
Osati et al. (2022) Modeled the problem of integrated lot-sizing and scheduling in a flexible job-shop environment considering energy efficiency. They developed a mathematical model for the integrated optimization of scheduling and lot-sizing problems. The developed model presented in the form of a mixed integer nonlinear programming (MINLP). To find the best solution, the model solved by using GAMS software. An illustrative example was presented and problems of different sizes were solved to assess the computational performance of the proposed model.
The literature reviewed above highlights a gap in research related to the joint consideration of lot-sizing, flexible job-shop scheduling, and remanufacturing, with a lack of studies addressing all three simultaneously. This paper proposes a model that integrates these three problems to minimize production, remanufacturing, and energy costs. The model also considers energy efficiency in the integrated lot-sizing and scheduling of a flexible job-shop problem. To solve large scale problems, three metaheuristic algorithms (GA, WOA and PSO algorithms) are used. Additionally, the model incorporates sequence-dependent modeling as a research gap to provide a more accurate representation of real-world scenarios.

Zarrouk et al. (2019) present a two-stage particle swarm optimization to solve flexible job-shop scheduling with a boundary-checking strategy for efficiency. Zhang et al. (2020) propose a two-stage framework reducing scheduling decision variables in flexible job-shops. Hajibabaei and Behnamian (2021) examine flexible resources impact on scheduling with parallel machines, using linear models and comparing with genetic algorithms aided by Tabu Search for larger instances. Osati et al. (2022) tackle integrated lot-sizing and scheduling in a flexible job-shop, emphasizing energy efficiency, through a MINLP model solved by GAMS software, filling a gap by marrying lot-sizing, scheduling, and remanufacturing, and implementing metaheuristics (GA, WOA, PSO) with sequence-dependent modeling for real-world applicability.

summarize this: The literature reviewed above highlights a gap in research related to the joint consideration of lot-sizing, flexible job-shop scheduling, and remanufacturing, with a lack of studies addressing all three simultaneously. This paper proposes a model that integrates these three problems to minimize production, remanufacturing, and energy costs. The model also considers energy efficiency in the integrated lot-sizing and scheduling of a flexible job-shop problem. To solve large scale problems, three metaheuristic algorithms (GA, WOA and PSO algorithms) are used. Additionally, the model incorporates sequence-dependent modeling as a research gap to provide a more accurate representation of real-world scenarios.

This paper proposes an integrated model that combines lot-sizing, scheduling, and remanufacturing to minimize costs and improve energy efficiency, utilizing metaheuristic algorithms and sequence-dependent modeling to reflect real-world conditions.

the research title is :Identifying and ranking the critical success factors of integrating CRM systems with artificial intelligence and knowledge management system using ISM. give a brief summary of chapters 1 (introduction), chapters 2 (literature review), and chapter 3 (methodology).

Without specific content from these chapters, I can offer you a general idea of what each might cover given the research title:

Chapter 1: Introduction
This chapter likely introduces the central research topic, explaining the importance of integrating Customer Relationship Management (CRM) systems with Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Knowledge Management Systems (KMS). It may discuss the reasoning behind the research, the potential benefits of such integration for organizations, and outline the critical success factors to be identified and ranked. The chapter probably states the research questions, objectives, and potential implications of the study. 

Chapter 2: Literature Review
The literature review chapter probably provides an in-depth examination of existing research on CRM systems, AI, and KMS. It likely explores how these systems have been used both individually and in combination. The review might focus on case studies, theoretical models, and success factors previously identified in scholarly literature, creating a base for understanding where the research gaps are and justifying the need for the current study.

Chapter 3: Methodology
This chapter would describe the research design and methods used to identify and rank the critical success factors. It may include descriptions of the Interpretable Structural Modeling (ISM) method, data collection and analysis procedures, the criteria for selecting experts or participants, and how the validity and reliability of the findings will be ensured. Additionally, this section might outline the steps taken to develop the ISM model and how it will be used to prioritize the success factors for integrating CRM systems with AI and KMS. 

Remember, this is a high-level summary based on typical chapter contents for a dissertation given the provided title. For an accurate summary, one would need to review the actual chapters.
research title is: Modeling of enhanced index tracking using goal programming and metaheuristics. give a paragraph for introduction of chapter 2

In Chapter 2, we delve into the extensive body of literature surrounding the concept of index tracking, an investment strategy aimed at replicating the performance of a particular financial market index. We scrutinize various methodologies traditionally employed in index tracking, focusing on the role of goal programming as an optimization technique to fine-tune portfolio selections. To complement these insights, we explore the integration of metaheuristic algorithms into index tracking models, which has emerged as a powerful means of navigating the complex, multidimensional search for near-optimal solutions in large, unpredictable investment landscapes. Case studies and previous research highlight the synthesis of goal programming with metaheuristics, which together enhance index tracking performances under diverse market conditions. This chapter sets the stage for our proposed model by critically analyzing the successes and limitations of existing strategies, thereby establishing the theoretical foundation for further innovations in enhanced index tracking.
give similar introduction for chapter 1

Chapter 1 serves as the gateway to our investigation into the cutting-edge realm of enhanced index tracking. This introductory chapter outlines the motivations for improving index tracking strategies, crucial for investors aiming to mirror the performance of benchmark indices while minimizing tracking error and managing costs effectively. We introduce the concept of goal programming as a pivotal optimization framework and discuss the emerging role of metaheuristics in transcending the limitations of traditional optimization techniques. The chapter sets out the research objectives, explicates the significance of the study in the contemporary financial landscape, and previews the methodological innovations our work introduces. By the chapter's conclusion, readers will comprehend the urgent need for advanced models in index tracking and be prepared for the in-depth exploration of existing literature and methodologies that follow.

summarize this:
فصل مقدمه یک پایان نامه نقش مهمی در تنظیم زمینه برای مطالعه دارد. هدف این فصل ارائه یک نمای کلی از پیشینه، منطق، سؤالات تحقیق و اهداف است. همچنین اهمیت تحقیق و پیامدهای بالقوه یافته‌های مطالعه را برجسته می‌کند.
در چارچوب ا شناسایی و رتبه بندی عوامل کلیدی موفقیت یکپارچه سازی سیستم های CRM با هوش مصنوعی و سیستم مدیریت دانش، فصل مقدمه به عنوان دروازه‌ای برای تحقیق عمل می‌کند، مقدمه‌ای برای موضوع ارائه می‌دهد و زمینه را برای فصل های بعدی فراهم می‌کند.
فصل مقدمه با معرفی موضوع تحقیق و ارائه پیشینه مختصری از صنعت داروسازی در ایران آغاز می شود. سپس اهمیت شناسایی و رتبه بندی عوامل کلیدی موفقیت یکپارچه سازی سیستم های CRM با هوش مصنوعی و سیستم مدیریت دانش را برجسته می‌کند. سپس این فصل به تشریح سوالات و اهداف تحقیق می پردازد و چارچوب روشنی برای مطالعه ارائه می دهد. همچنین روشی را که مطالعه به کار خواهد گرفت برجسته می کند.
به طور کلی، فصل مقدمه این پایان نامه مروری واضح و مختصر از پیشینه، منطق، سؤالات تحقیق و اهداف تحقیق ارائه می دهد. این فصل زمینه را برای فصل های بعدی آماده می کند.

The introduction chapter of the dissertation plays a critical role in setting the stage for the study. It aims to provide an overview of the background, rationale, research questions, and objectives. It also highlights the importance of the research and the potential implications of the study's findings. Specifically, in the context of identifying and ranking the key success factors of CRM system integration with artificial intelligence and knowledge management systems, the introduction chapter acts as a gateway to the research, setting up for subsequent chapters. Starting with an introduction to the research topic and a brief history of the pharmaceutical industry in Iran, the chapter emphasizes the significance of recognizing and ranking the key success factors of CRM system integration. The chapter goes on to detail the research questions and objectives, laying a clear framework for the study and underscoring the methodology to be employed. Overall, the introduction chapter provides a clear and concise review of the background, rationale, research questions, and objectives, preparing the ground for the following chapters.

Define These:
AI, Knowledge Management, CRM, Critical Success Factor

AI (Artificial Intelligence): AI refers to the simulation of human intelligence in machines that are designed to think and learn like humans. The term encompasses any machine that exhibits traits associated with a human mind, such as learning, problem-solving, and decision-making.

Knowledge Management: Knowledge Management is the process of creating, sharing, using, and managing the knowledge and information of an organization. It refers to a multidisciplinary approach to achieving organizational objectives by making the best use of knowledge.

CRM (Customer Relationship Management): CRM is a technology for managing all your company's relationships and interactions with current and potential customers. It aims to improve business relationships to grow your business.

Critical Success Factor: Critical Success Factors are the crucial steps companies perform to achieve their goals and objectives and implement their strategies. These factors are vital for a business's or project's success and are used to define the elements necessary for an organization to achieve its mission.

summarize this:
این فصل مروری جامع بر تحقیقات و مطالعات موجود در زمینه شناسایی و رتبه بندی عوامل کلیدی موفقیت یکپارچه سازی سیستم های CRM با هوش مصنوعی و سیستم مدیریت دانش ارائه می‌کند.
هدف این فصل مشخص کردن شکاف تحقیق و سؤالات تحقیقی است که مطالعه قصد دارد به آنها بپردازد. این فصل همچنین اهمیت شناسایی و رتبه بندی عوامل کلیدی موفقیت یکپارچه سازی سیستم های CRM با هوش مصنوعی و سیستم مدیریت دانش را برجسته می‌کند. مرور ادبیات تحلیلی انتقادی از ادبیات موجود در مورد شناسایی و رتبه بندی عوامل کلیدی موفقیت یکپارچه سازی سیستم های CRM با هوش مصنوعی و سیستم مدیریت دانش ارائه می‌کند. همچنین مفهوم مدلسازی ساختاری تفسیری، یک تکنیک قدرتمند برای تعیین ارتباط بین عوامل را مورد بحث قرار می‌دهد.
به طور کلی، فصل مرور ادبیات با هدف ارائه درک جامعی از مفاهیم کلیدی، نظریه‌ها و روش‌شناسی مرتبط با سؤالات تحقیق مطالعه است. همچنین شکاف‌های موجود در ادبیات و نیاز به تحقیقات بیشتر در زمینه یکپارچه سازی سیستم های CRM با هوش مصنوعی و سیستم مدیریت دانش را برجسته می‌کند.

This chapter provides a comprehensive review of existing research and studies on identifying and ranking the critical success factors for integrating CRM systems with artificial intelligence and knowledge management systems. It aims to identify research gaps and questions that the study intends to address, highlighting the importance of recognizing and ranking these critical success factors. The chapter offers a critical analytical literature review on the subject and discusses Interpretative Structural Modeling, a powerful technique for determining the interrelationships between factors. Overall, the literature review is intended to give a complete understanding of the key concepts, theories, and methodology related to the research questions, while also emphasizing existing gaps in the literature and the need for further research in the integration of CRM systems with AI and knowledge management.
what are the most important aspects of CRM?

The most important aspects of Customer Relationship Management (CRM) include:

1. Customer Data Management: Collecting, organizing, and managing customer information is vital to understand customer behaviors and preferences.

2. Customer Interaction Tracking: Recording interactions with customers across various touchpoints to provide a unified customer experience and to serve them better.

3. Personalization: Tailoring offerings and communications to individual customer needs and preferences to enhance customer satisfaction and loyalty.

4. Customer Support: Providing timely and effective service and support to address customer inquiries, issues, and feedback.

5. Sales Management: Streamlining and automating the sales process, from lead generation to closing sales and post-sale follow-up.

6. Marketing Automation: Using CRM tools to automate marketing campaigns, track their efficiency, and adjust strategies based on data analysis.

7. Analytics and Reporting: Employing advanced analytics to gain insights into customer data which help in informed decision-making and strategic planning.

8. Integration Capabilities: Seamlessly integrating with other systems, such as marketing, sales, and services, for synchronized business operations.

9. Customer Segmentation: Grouping customers into segments based on common characteristics to enhance targeted marketing efforts.

10. Workflow Automation: Automating routine tasks to improve efficiency, reduce human error, and enable employees to focus on more strategic activities.

11. Customer Retention and Loyalty Programs: Developing strategies to retain customers and encourage repeat business through loyalty programs and incentives.

Each of these facets is critical for ensuring that CRM objectives—namely, building lasting customer relationships, optimizing customer interactions, and driving sales—are achieved effectively.
